comment_text:
😡👎 This season was an absolute disappointment. Netflix destroyed what once made Love, Death & Robots great. This volume lacks soul, risk, vision… everything.

I saw someone comment that “people just want blood and fights.” No — that’s not what this show was about. What made it special was the clever satire of “Three Robots,” the brutal beauty and mind-blowing ending of “The Witness,” or the raw, unmatched cyberpunk power of “Sonnie’s Edge,” which, in my opinion, remains unbeatable.

This season? Maybe “Mini Alien Invasion” was okay, and one or two others, but with pathetic runtimes of 7 minutes? Seriously? Is this all that’s left of a series that was supposed to push the boundaries of animation and storytelling?

A total mess. I honestly wish this season didn’t exist.

comment_text:
mixing a zombie woman, cookies, and magic! Here are some Love, Death & Robots inspired episode ideas based on that combo:


---

**1. The Cookie Curse
Style: Dark fantasy with horror and humor
In a post-apocalyptic world, a zombie woman stumbles upon an enchanted cookie recipe that, when baked and eaten, has the power to reverse the effects of zombification. But there’s a catch: the magic of the cookies comes with a price. As she bakes batch after batch, the cookies become more powerful, and she begins to regain her human form—only to realize that the magic is slowly consuming her soul. With every cookie, the line between life and death grows blurrier. Now, she must choose whether to continue baking and risk losing her humanity or give up the cookies and live with her undead curse forever.


---

**2. The Zombie Baker
Style: Whimsical and eerie animation
A zombie woman works in a small bakery in a ruined city, baking the most delicious cookies anyone has ever tasted. But there's a secret ingredient: magic. Each cookie has the power to bring people back from the dead—but only temporarily. When a customer eats a cookie, they return for a short while, able to converse and reconnect with the living. However, they slowly begin to decay again, and the zombie baker is left with the agony of saying goodbye over and over. She must uncover the truth behind the magic of the cookies before it drives her mad.


---

**3. Cookie of the Undead
Style: Gritty, action-packed animation
A powerful sorceress, cursed with eternal undeath, discovers a magical cookie that can grant her the power to control the minds of the living. But when she bites into the cookie, she inadvertently becomes the target of a rogue group of resistance fighters who seek to put an end to her reign. As they battle through a dark and decaying city, they realize that the cookie’s magic is connected to an ancient spell that can either bring about the end of the zombie apocalypse or plunge the world into eternal darkness. The sorceress must decide whether she will use the magic for destruction or to free humanity once and for all.


---

**4. The Undead Confectioner
Style: Surreal, magical realism
In a world where magic and the undead coexist, a zombie woman works as a confectioner, crafting magical cookies that hold mysterious powers. These cookies can grant wishes, alter fate, or even revive the dead—each with an unpredictable twist. One day, a young boy discovers her bakery and asks for a cookie that can bring his sister back to life. As the zombie woman reluctantly agrees to bake the wish cookie, she reveals the dark price of magic: every wish comes with a sacrifice. But the boy is determined, and as the cookies begin to crumble, the consequences become more disastrous than he ever imagined.


---

**5. The Witch's Crumble
Style: Dark comedy with magical elements
A zombie woman, turned into a reluctant witch after an accident with an experimental magic potion, stumbles upon an enchanted cookie recipe. With her new-found abilities, she bakes cookies that can temporarily turn back time—reversing the moments when she was alive. However, every time she uses the magic, she risks becoming more and more "undead" until she’s completely consumed by the curse. The episode follows her chaotic and humorous attempts to fix her mistakes, bake the perfect cookies, and stop herself from becoming a full-fledged zombie forever.


---

**6. Cookie of the Forgotten
Style: Emotional, eerie animation
In a world where the dead cannot remember their lives, a zombie woman who has retained some of her memories discovers a magical cookie that can restore lost memories—if eaten. She begins baking these cookies and handing them out to other zombies, hoping to restore their past lives. As memories flood back, however, the restored individuals begin to realize that they were once monstrous, and the guilt they feel makes them violent and dangerous. Now, the zombie woman must decide whether to continue restoring memories and risk unleashing chaos, or to stop the magic and embrace her own forgotten past.

comment_text:
mix of love, cheese, and a frisbee! Here are some Love, Death & Robots inspired episode ideas based on that combination:


---

**1. Cheese of the Heart
Style: Whimsical, light-hearted animation with a touch of romance
In a world where emotions are stored in physical objects, one man’s heart is symbolized by a rare, enchanted wheel of cheese. When his heart is accidentally stolen by a mysterious figure, the man sets out on a wild journey to retrieve it. Along the way, he must navigate a quirky landscape filled with love, humor, and strange characters, including a talking frisbee that once belonged to the goddess of love. But with each throw of the frisbee, he learns more about his true feelings and what it takes to heal his heart.


---

**2. The Cheddar Wars
Style: Action-packed with a quirky, comedic edge
In a future where cheese is the most valuable resource, entire civilizations fight to control the world’s supply of the rarest cheeses. The protagonist, a lovable but clumsy hero, stumbles upon a mystical frisbee that can slice through any material—particularly cheese. As he learns to wield it, he becomes a symbol of hope for a cheese-less world, finding love and allies in the most unexpected places. But the enemy factions are ruthless, and they’ll stop at nothing to capture the frisbee, believing it to be the key to ultimate cheese domination.


---

**3. Love and Curds
Style: Romantic and surreal animation
Two unlikely souls meet on a peculiar planet where everything is made of cheese. The duo shares an inexplicable connection, and every time they express affection for each other, a new kind of cheese is created. But a strange rule of the planet says that when someone shows love, they must also give up a part of themselves. As their connection grows, they must sacrifice their most beloved cheeses and navigate a world of bizarre, melting landscapes where love, cheese, and frisbees play an unexpected role in saving their home.


---

**4. Frisbee of Fate
Style: Fantasy adventure with a touch of humor
A young hero discovers an ancient frisbee that can alter the course of destiny. This frisbee was once used by the goddess of love to bring people together or tear them apart. When the frisbee accidentally hits a wheel of divine cheese, a spark of magic triggers the hero’s hidden abilities, giving them the power to influence emotions and bond people. As they travel through a cheese-infused world, they must avoid the wrath of the Cheese Gods and use their newfound abilities to save the world from an emotional catastrophe.


---

**5. The Cheddar Lovers
Style: Light-hearted with a touch of sci-fi
In a world where love is expressed through food, a pair of star-crossed lovers communicates through their favorite cheese dishes. But when a rival couple invents a frisbee that can create new cheese flavors, their love is put to the test. As they compete in a cheesy battle to win the hearts of the masses, they discover the true meaning of love is not in the cheese or frisbee, but in the act of creating and sharing it with someone special.


---

**6. Melted Hearts
Style: Emotional and dream-like animation
A heartbroken young woman stumbles upon a magical frisbee that can bring people back together—if they throw it with true love in their heart. She uses the frisbee to visit the memories of her past relationships, each one centered around a specific cheese that represented a moment in her life. As she relives these moments, she learns about herself, her heartache, and the true nature of love—slowly realizing that the key to happiness might lie in something as simple as a shared slice of cheese and a frisbee throw.

comment_text:
mixing parachutes, UFOs, and ships! Here are some Love, Death & Robots inspired episode ideas based on that combo:


---

**1. The Skyfall Paradox
Style: High-action, sci-fi 3D animation
A massive, intergalactic warship is forced to evacuate its crew after a disastrous crash-landing on a distant, uncharted planet. The crew members are scattered across the hostile terrain, each equipped with parachutes meant to guide them to safety. But as they fall, they are pursued by UFOs—alien ships intent on capturing them for unknown experiments. The survivors must navigate through the chaotic environment, dodging both the UFOs and the planet’s dangerous wildlife, to regroup and escape before the planet's atmosphere collapses on them.


---

**2. The Ship Above
Style: Dark, atmospheric animation with horror elements
A spaceship travels through a distant galaxy and discovers an abandoned, seemingly haunted ship drifting in space. As the crew boards, they discover it’s not empty: it’s a colossal UFO that has been stranded for centuries. As they investigate, they encounter a deadly force that starts picking them off one by one. In their final moments, the crew must figure out how to escape using parachutes to leap into space and avoid being pulled into the ship's gravitational hold. However, an unknown entity might just have other plans for them.


---

**3. The Floating Citadel
Style: Steampunk-inspired, adventurous animation
On a ship floating high above a storm-ravaged planet, a group of explorers are tasked with retrieving an ancient artifact that is said to control the weather. As they parachute down to the surface, they discover a UFO hidden beneath the storm clouds, inhabited by a strange alien civilization. The citizens have mastered the technology of weather manipulation and use the UFO as a ship and floating city, but something has gone horribly wrong, and they need the explorers’ help to stop a deadly storm system from swallowing the entire planet.


---

**4. Chase of the Sky Pirates
Style: Retro-futuristic action animation
A group of elite sky pirates in futuristic flying ships have hijacked a luxury cruiser and are fleeing into the clouds. The crew of the cruiser must use parachutes to jump onto the pirate ships, risking their lives to retake the vessel. As they fight for control, the crew must decide whether to ally with the pirates or stop them from bringing chaos to the galaxy.

As the crew parachutes into the waters below, they are faced with both the alien ship pursuing them and the strange, undersea world they must survive in. The deeper they dive, the more the artifact seems to have an influence, causing mysterious hallucinations and manipulating the ocean’s creatures. The crew must reach the artifact’s true location before the UFO captures them and drags them into the abyss.


---

**6. Whispers in the Clouds

A group of explorers crash-lands on a massive, floating island high in the sky, but their ship is instantly taken by a fleet of UFOs. To survive, they are forced to parachute into unknown territory, where they discover a mysterious and ancient civilization that has mastered both aerial ships and UFO technology. But as they navigate the floating city, they realize the inhabitants are not just alien beings, but echoes of long-lost souls trapped in an eternal storm. The explorers must uncover the secret of the floating city or risk becoming part of its never-ending cycle.
